title: Brachyglottis cassinioides
taxonomy: Brachyglottis cassinioides is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes many species of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or asters. This classification is part of the larger group of plants known as angiosperms, or flowering plants. Brachyglottis cassinioides belongs to the genus Brachyglottis, which is a group of plants native to New Zealand. The genus Brachyglottis is often placed within the subfamily Asteroideae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Brachyglottis cassinioides is further classified as a species within the genus Brachyglottis.

intro: Brachyglottis cassinioides, also known as the New Zealand daisy, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Brachyglottis cassinioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and skin irritations.
